Shipped Projects

THORN — Parametric Lighting System
View Project

Thorn is a Red Dot Award–winning modular ceiling light by QZENS, constructed from two primary component types. To operationalize this design for production, I developed a parametric generation framework in Blender using Geometry Nodes and Blender API. The system encodes the assembly logic directly into the node structure, allowing controlled variation of length, density, and branching frequency while preserving structural constraints. A custom export add-on bridges the model to fabrication documentation that automatically compiles indexed component counts, part typologies, and configuration-specific production data directly from the geometry. The result is a computational design-to-production pipeline where variation, visualization, and installation logic coexist within a single system — transforming a sculptural object into a scalable, rule-based product architecture ready for launch.

0 Days Without Accidents
View on Devpost

An award-winning endless runner that received 1st Place at the Devpost Hackathon. Set in Station 496, the game follows a newly hired technician who accidentally triggers an explosion and must navigate an unstable facility while recovering his scattered succulents. The project was conceived as a technical exploration of constraint-based procedural generation inspired by wave function collapse principles. Rather than implementing the algorithm directly, I translated its underlying ideas—adjacency rules, compatibility states, and weighted randomness into a modular tile-based generation framework. Each segment encodes spatial constraints and collision logic allowing the runtime system to assemble levels dynamically without producing invalid or unsolvable configurations. The result is a compact system-driven endless runner where procedural generation function as a cohesive framework shaped within the constraints of a hackathon setting.

NLE Batch Editor
View on Github

NLA Batch Editor is a GNU-licensed, open-source Blender add-on focused on improving productivity inside the NLA Editor. Originally built as a personal workflow tool, it evolved into a community-ready project that enables batch pushdown, track and strip management, advanced selection tools, and large-scale animation cleanup. It aims to remove manual rivalry from animation workflows and provide a structured foundation for scalable, web-ready exports. The project is open for collaboration and future community-driven enhancements.

Azuki: Elemental AR Experience
View Project

An immersive augmented reality experience developed to promote Azuki’s merchandise through character-driven environmental effects. Inspired by Azuki’s character universe, I designed and developed four real-time VFX ecosystem — each dynamically tracks, orbits, and responds to the user. The system used body tracking as a spatial anchor, generating real-time procedural effects centered around the user. I built custom GPU shaders to orchestrate particle systems and vertex displacement across four elemental effects. Procedural systems controlled the swirl, speed, and reactive movement of the particles, creating organic whirlpool effects around the user. The project required careful optimization of draw calls and particle counts to maintain high visual fidelity within mobile AR performance constraints.
